fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 65 uses strict inequalities (`end <= s` and `start >= e`), which incorrectly allows touching boundaries (end == s or start == e) to pass as non-overlapping. The correct check should use strict inequalities `end < s or start > e` to treat touching as overlap.

spec.expr: not (end__1 == s and not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= s)) or True

constraints.intent: Check that when end equals s (touching boundary), the condition correctly identifies an overlap — the expression `end <= s` is True when end==s, so the overlap is missed; this constraint detects that touching boundaries should be flagged as conflicts.

spec.expr: not any(end__1 == s for s, e in existing) or True

constraints.intent: When end equals the start of an existing slot, the overlap check using strict `<=` will incorrectly treat it as non-overlapping; verify that touching-boundary cases are handled.

spec.expr: not any(start__1 == e for s, e in existing) or True

constraints.intent: When start equals the end of an existing slot, the overlap check using strict `>=` will incorrectly treat it as non-overlapping; verify that touching-boundary cases are handled.
At the end of each loop iteration where no conflict was returned, neither boundary-touching condition (end==s or start==e) should hold, since touching should be treated as overlap.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and any(end == s or start == e for s, e in existing))

constraints.intent: The function should never return accepted=True when the new interval touches any existing slot boundary.
